pr.title: Cleanup of a ton of data pointers using intns's tool and manual labels
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Merged cleanup PR whose title indicates broad cleanup of data pointers using intns's tooling plus manual labels. The available PR slice contains no changed files, diff excerpt, body, comments, or review records, so exact symbols, affected modules, and implementation details cannot be verified from this context.
what_changed: Cleaned up many data pointers, according to the PR title.
Used intns's tool for part of the cleanup, supplemented by manual labels.
No file-level or diff-level evidence is available in the provided slice, so the specific data sections, symbols, or modules touched are unknown.
smart_moves: Combined automated pointer-identification tooling with manual label work, which is a sensible workflow for broad data cleanup where tool output still needs human validation.
Focused on data pointer cleanup as a separate PR, making later decompilation and symbolization work easier to search and reason about.
decomp_lessons: For large-scale data cleanup, tooling can accelerate pointer discovery, but manual labels remain important for clarity and correctness.
Even cleanup-only PRs should ideally preserve changed-file and diff context for future archaeology; this slice lacks enough evidence to reconstruct exact technical changes.
Labeling data pointers early can improve later matching/decomp work by replacing raw addresses or anonymous data with meaningful references.
naming_conventions: The PR title references use of manual labels, but no actual label names are present in the available context.
No concrete naming convention can be extracted from this slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use specialized tooling to identify or clean up data pointers at scale.
Follow automated pointer cleanup with manual label assignment where tool output lacks semantic names.
No specific assembly directives, relocation patterns, or matching tricks are visible in the provided PR data.
